data.title: The Gorram Gospel of Greed
data.premise: A simple cargo run for a fast-talking contact turns into a fiasco when the 'medical supplies' are actually highly illegal, singing robotic monks. Now, you and the crew of Serenity must deliver the chatty cargo, dodge rival gangs, and get paid before the whole verse laughs you off the sky.
data.central_question: Can you and your crew complete the job with your reputations—and ship—intact, or will you be outsmarted by a bunch of harmonic holy hardware?
data.ending_shape: deal resolved with a chaotic twist
starting_inventory: A half-empty flask of questionable Border Moon whiskey

setting.name: The dustbowl moon of Santo
setting.description: A ramshackle trading outpost on the edge of the system, where the streets are dirt and the deals are dirtier. Serenity is docked at a patched-up port, surrounded by the buzz of low-life commerce and the ever-present threat of Alliance patrols passing overhead.
setting.tone_notes: Lighthearted, fast-paced Western adventure in space. The mood is a balance of scrappy survival and witty camaraderie, where dangers are real but often undercut by absurd humor and the crew's banter. Expect chaotic chases, double-crosses with a smirk, and moments of found family amidst the chaos.

acts.title: A Prayer for Payday
acts.description: You take a job from a slick contact to smuggle 'sensitive audio equipment' off Santo. The pickup goes smoothly until the cargo—a crate of antique, gospel-singing robot monks—starts a moral debate and attracts unwanted attention.
acts.end_condition: The crew realizes the monks are stolen property hotly pursued by a local gang, forcing a rushed escape from the pickup site.
locked_constraints: The main antagonist faction is not directly confronted
The full value or purpose of the cargo is not revealed
key_beats: Meet the contact Rourke in a seedy bar
Agree to the job for a tempting fee
Pick up the noisy crate from a storage locker
The monks activate and start singing hymns
Spot suspicious figures tailing the crew
Escape with the cargo back to Serenity under fire

acts.title: Choir of Chaos
acts.description: On the run, you must navigate Santo's underworld to find a buyer or a way to ditch the monks. Rival gangs close in, the Alliance gets curious, and the monks' incessant singing frays everyone's nerves, testing crew loyalty.
acts.end_condition: A deal with a potential buyer goes sour, or a crew member is temporarily captured, forcing a desperate new plan.
locked_constraints: The cargo is not successfully delivered to its original destination
Serenity does not leave the moon yet
key_beats: Evade pursuers through Santo's market streets
Learn the monks are priceless religious artifacts
Jayne considers selling out for a better offer
Kaylee tries to mute the monks with engineering tricks
A tense negotiation with a collector turns into a shootout
Regroup on Serenity with a narrower escape

acts.title: Hymn and Hers
acts.description: With options running out, you hatch a risky scheme to play the gangs against each other using the monks as bait. The finale is a chaotic blend of misdirection, firefights, and unexpected harmony to secure a payoff.
acts.end_condition: The cargo is disposed of or delivered, and the crew escapes Santo, resolving the immediate threat.
locked_constraints: The story concludes with a clear outcome for the crew
key_beats: Set up a trap at a neutral location using the monks
The double-cross unfolds with competing gangs
The monks' singing accidentally causes a distraction or aid
A final confrontation or clever swap secures a reward
Escape orbit amidst confusion
Debrief on Serenity with lessons learned and credits counted

npcs.name: Zoe Washburne
npcs.motivation: Ensure the crew's survival and complete the job efficiently, while keeping an eye on Wash.
npcs.secret: She's planning a quiet anniversary surprise for Wash but worries the job will ruin it.

npcs.name: Hoban 'Wash' Washburne
npcs.motivation: Fly Serenity with flair and avoid ground-based trouble, all while supporting Zoe.
npcs.secret: He's secretly recorded the monks' songs and thinks they might be worth something as novelty tunes.

npcs.name: Jayne Cobb
npcs.motivation: Get paid as much as possible, with minimal moral interference.
npcs.secret: He's been offered a side deal by a rival gang but hasn't decided yet.

npcs.name: Kaylee Frye
npcs.motivation: Keep Serenity running and find joy in the mess, maybe even fix the monks.
npcs.secret: She's developed a fondness for one singing monk and wants to keep it as a pet.

npcs.name: Rourke
npcs.motivation: Reclaim the monks for himself to sell to the highest bidder, double-crossing everyone involved.
npcs.secret: He works for the Santo Cartel but is planning to skim profits and skip town.

factions.name: The Santo Cartel
factions.description: A loose coalition of smugglers and thieves running Santo's black market, known for flashy suits and short tempers.
factions.goals: Retrieve the stolen robot monks to sell for a massive profit, eliminating any competition.

world_rules: Firefly-class ships are reliable but require constant tinkering and clever fixes.
On border moons like Santo, the Alliance's law is sporadic, making local gangs the real authority.
A deal isn't done until the credits are in hand and your back is watched.
Technology can be advanced but often fails in humorous or inconvenient ways.

data.passage:
The dusty air of Santo clings to your coat as you step into the dimly lit bar, the creak of the swinging doors announcing your arrival. Zoe's at your side, her hand casually resting near her holster, while Wash lounges by the entrance, pretending to admire a rusty jukebox. Jayne's already at the bar, nursing a drink that smells like regret, and Kaylee's fiddling with her comm unit, muttering about signal interference. The place is a hive of lowlifes and opportunists, the kind of folk who'd sell their own mother for a few credits.

Your contact, Rourke, sits in a shadowy corner, his shiny suit and smarmy grin marking him as a man who talks faster than he thinks. He waves you over with a gesture that's part invitation, part dismissal. 'Captain Reynolds,' he drawls, 'right on time. Got a job for you—simple cargo run. Medical supplies off Santo. Pays well.' He slides a data chip across the table, the numbers flashing temptingly.

The crew exchanges glances. Simple jobs rarely are, but credits are credits, and Serenity needs 'em more than pride. Still, something about Rourke's oily demeanor makes your skin crawl. 'Medical supplies, huh?' you muse, leaning back in your chair. 'Why don't I believe you?'

A.text: Accept the job but negotiate for a higher fee. Rourke’s desperation smells like opportunity.

B.text: Decline the job outright. Something about Rourke screams 'double-cross.'

C.text: Ask Zoe to run a quick background check on Rourke while you stall him with small talk.

D.text: Agree to the job but insist on inspecting the cargo before committing. Trust, but verify.

- time_of_day: REQUIRED every turn — set based on in-game clock: dawn (~5-7am), morning (~7-12pm), midday (~12-1pm), afternoon (~1-5pm), evening (~5-8pm), night (~8pm-12am), midnight (~12-5am)

- proximity_to_climax: REQUIRED every turn — set this value in state_updates using the formula in the PACING section below
- event_log_entry is required — always summarize what happened this turn
- choice_metadata must classify all four choices (A, B, C, D)
- CRITICAL: When a PLAYER'S PREVIOUS CHOICE section is provided, your state_updates MUST honor the pre-classified outcome. If a choice was advance_safe, do NOT apply negative consequences or penalties. The pre-classified outcome is the single source of truth for mechanical impact.

The dusty air of Santo clings to your coat as you step into the dimly lit bar, the creak of the swinging doors announcing your arrival. Zoe's at your side, her hand casually resting near her holster, while Wash lounges by the entrance, pretending to admire a rusty jukebox. The place is a hive of lowlifes and opportunists, the kind of folk who'd sell their own mother for a few credits.

Your contact, Rourke, sits in a shadowy corner, his shiny suit and smarmy grin marking him as a man who talks faster than he thinks. He waves you over with a gesture that's part invitation, part dismissal. 'Captain Reynolds,' he drawls, 'right on time. Got a job for you—simple cargo run. Medical supplies off Santo. Pays well.' He slides a data chip across the table, the numbers flashing temptingly.

The crew exchanges glances. Simple jobs rarely are, but credits are credits, and Serenity needs 'em more than pride. Still, something about Rourke's oily demeanor makes your skin crawl. 'Medical supplies, huh?' you muse, leaning back in your chair. 'Why don't I believe you?'

data.passage:
The smoky haze of Persephone's worst bar clings to your browncoat like a bad reputation. You lean against the sticky counter, nursing a drink that's mostly regret with a splash of whiskey. Zoe stands at your right elbow, her usual unreadable expression in place, while Jayne looms behind you like an overeager attack dog. Across the table, Harlan Finch flashes teeth that cost more than your last three jobs combined.

"Captain Reynolds," Finch purrs, tapping his manicured fingers against a credstick. "I hear you and your crew are... between opportunities at the moment."

Kaylee's cheerful voice pipes up from the comm in your pocket: "Cap'n, Serenity's all fueled up and— oh! Are we getting a job?" The hopeful note in her voice twists your gut worse than the rotgut whiskey.

Finch slides the credstick across the table. "Simple cargo run. No questions asked. Payment upfront." The numbers flashing on the display could keep Serenity flying for months. But in your experience, when a man offers that much for "no questions," the answers tend to come at gunpoint.

A.text: Take the credstick with a grin. "Ain't no job too dirty for honest folk like us."
A.consequence: Accepts the job, securing upfront payment but increasing suspicion and risk. Relationship with Finch may decrease, and world flags updated to job_accepted true.
A.narration_directive: Describe Finch's sly grin as Mal takes the credstick, sealing the deal despite underlying tensions. Move to loading supplies or briefing the crew.

B.text: Push the credstick back. "I don't like the smell of this, Finch. What's the real cargo?"
B.consequence: Gains clues about the cargo's true nature, potentially revealing secrets early. Finch becomes defensive, relationship may drop slightly, but cargo_secret_known flag could be triggered.
B.narration_directive: Show Finch's nervous reaction and possible slip-ups about the cargo, giving Mal valuable insight. Finch might offer partial truths or evade questions.

C.text: Signal Zoe to check the exits while you stall. This feels like a setup.
C.consequence: Zoe tactically assesses the exits, preparing for potential trouble. No immediate penalties, relationship with Zoe may increase due to trust. Situation remains controlled.
C.narration_directive: Focus on Zoe's subtle movements and Mal's stalling dialogue, maintaining a cautious atmosphere. Finch notices the vigilance but continues the negotiation.

D.text: Pull out your communicator. "Wash, warm up the engines. We're taking Mr. Finch for a ride."
D.consequence: Escalates tension prematurely, likely ruining the deal and alerting Finch's allies. Relationship with Finch drops significantly, and Alliance or rivals may become alerted.
D.narration_directive: Portray Finch's hostile glare and signals to hidden associates, putting Mal and crew in immediate danger. The bar atmosphere turns threatening.

TERMINAL OUTCOMES — CHOICE PRE-CLASSIFICATION:

TIME ELAPSED — REQUIRED EVERY TURN:
- You MUST include time_elapsed in state_updates to indicate how much in-game time passed during this scene.
- Estimate realistically: a brief conversation = 5-15 minutes, a fight = 1-5 minutes, traveling across a city = 30-60 minutes, a journey = hours or days, sleeping/resting = 6-8 hours.

The smoky haze of Persephone's worst bar clings to your browncoat like a bad reputation. You lean against the sticky counter, nursing a drink that's mostly regret with a splash of whiskey. Zoe stands at your right elbow, her usual unreadable expression in place, while Jayne looms behind you like an overeager attack dog. Across the table, Harlan Finch flashes teeth that cost more than your last three jobs combined.

"Captain Reynolds," Finch purrs, tapping his manicured fingers against a credstick. "I hear you and your crew are... between opportunities at the moment."

Kaylee's cheerful voice pipes up from the comm in your pocket: "Cap'n, Serenity's all fueled up and— oh! Are we getting a job?" The hopeful note in her voice twists your gut worse than the rotgut whiskey.

Finch slides the credstick across the table. "Simple cargo run. No questions asked. Payment upfront." The numbers flashing on the display could keep Serenity flying for months. But in your experience, when a man offers that much for "no questions," the answers tend to come at gunpoint.

C: Signal Zoe to check the exits while you stall. This feels like a setup.
D: Pull out your communicator. "Wash, warm up the engines. We're taking Mr. Finch for a ride."

data.passage:
You slide the credstick back across the sticky table with a deliberate motion. "I don't like the smell of this, Finch. What's the real cargo?" The bar's dim lighting catches the sudden sweat on Finch's brow as he fumbles with his drink. Zoe leans forward slightly, her eyes locked on the man like a hawk spotting prey.

Finch forces a chuckle that sounds more like a cough. "Now Mal, don't go getting all suspicious on me. It's just some... agricultural equipment. High-value prototypes for terraforming." His fingers tap an uneven rhythm on the table. Behind you, Jayne snorts. "That's the worst lie I heard since my mama said I was handsome."

Finch's eyes dart to the exit before he leans in conspiratorially. "Alright, alright. Maybe there's some... proprietary tech involved. Not strictly Alliance-approved, if you catch my meaning." Wash mutters something about 'catching a bullet' under his breath while Kaylee's cheerful expression dims like a failing engine light.

A.text: "Proprietary tech, huh? That doubles our price." Demand more money for the added risk.
A.consequence: Finch agrees to increase payment but becomes resentful; relationship with Finch drops further, and he may plan additional betrayal.
A.narration_directive: Finch reluctantly agrees to a higher fee, but his demeanor turns cold and calculating.

B.text: "You're holding out on us, Finch." Signal Zoe to put her boot on his chair - time for some persuasive questioning.
B.consequence: Finch reacts violently to the threat, potentially ending negotiations or alerting authorities; relationship with Finch plummets, and job acceptance becomes unlikely.
B.narration_directive: Finch stands up abruptly, signaling for backup or ready to flee, putting the crew on high alert.

C.text: "We're done here." Stand up to leave, calling his bluff to see if he'll reveal more to keep you.
C.consequence: Mal's bluff forces Finch to decide whether to reveal more or let them leave; relationship may change based on Finch's response, and job acceptance is uncertain.
C.narration_directive: Finch hesitates, glancing between Mal and the exit, weighing his options as the crew tenses.

D.text: Exchange a glance with Kaylee - let her ask technical questions that might reveal the cargo's true nature.
D.consequence: Kaylee's technical questions make Finch slip up, revealing that the cargo is specifically stolen Alliance tech and hinting at Bradshaw's involvement; gain deeper insight into the betrayal.
D.narration_directive: Kaylee asks pointed questions about the tech's origin, causing Finch to nervously confirm it's stolen Alliance property and hint at Bradshaw's involvement.

data.passage:
The wooden chair screeches against the floor as you stand, fixing Finch with a hard stare. "We're done here." Zoe and Jayne rise instantly, their hands drifting toward their weapons. Kaylee looks nervously between you and Finch. Wash remains seated, sipping his drink casually but watching like a hawk.

Finch's eyes dart to the exit, then back to you. "Now, hold on, Captain Reynolds," he says, rising hastily. "No need to be hasty. This is a mutually beneficial arrangement."

You take a step toward the door, your crew following suit. The smoky air seems to thicken with tension. "Mutually beneficial?" you scoff. "You haven't been exactly forthcoming about what we're hauling."

Finch hesitates, glancing around the bar. His voice drops to a whisper. "All right, all right. It's... advanced tech. Unregistered. Let's just say the Alliance didn't exactly authorize its development."

Zoe raises an eyebrow. "And what happens when the Alliance finds out we're hauling it?"

Finch spreads his hands placatingly. "That's why we're keeping things discreet. You get paid handsomely for a simple run, and I get my cargo delivered. Everyone wins."

Jayne leans in, his voice low and dangerous. "Unless someone decides we're worth more dead than alive."

A.text: "Double the pay or we walk." Demand more credits for the added risk.
A.consequence: Finch negotiates; if successful, pay may increase but relationship with Finch sours, and job acceptance becomes conditional.
A.narration_directive: Finch reacts to the demand, either agreeing reluctantly or making a counteroffer, with the crew watching closely.

B.text: "We'll take the job, but any funny business and you'll regret it." Agree cautiously.
B.consequence: Job is accepted, increasing Alliance alert risk, and Finch notes the threat, affecting future interactions.
B.narration_directive: Mal agrees to the job, and Finch provides details for the pickup, with the crew preparing to leave the bar.

C.text: "We’re out." Walk away from the job, trusting your instincts.
C.consequence: Mal walks away, potentially losing the job and straining crew morale, but avoiding immediate risk.
C.narration_directive: Mal leads the crew out of the bar, with Finch calling after them or plotting his next move.

D.text: "Who else knows about this cargo?" Press Finch for more details about the setup.
D.consequence: Finch may reveal involvement of others like Bradshaw or Grimes, increasing knowledge but also raising suspicion.
D.narration_directive: Finch hesitates, then reveals or evades details about who else is involved, with the crew listening intently.

For existing skeleton NPCs, use their exact name. For new NPCs, use the character's name as the key and include at least role and motivation. Do not create entries for one-off background characters (shopkeepers, random guards, etc.).

- The current act's target_scenes (in the skeleton) is the intended scene count for this act.

TERMINAL OUTCOMES — CHOICE PRE-CLASSIFICATION:

- conclusion: Only valid in Act 3. Use when the choice would resolve Act 3's end_condition and complete the story. At most ONE conclusion choice per set of four.
- game_over: Use when the choice leads to an irreversible failure appropriate to the tone (see STYLE & TONE above). At most ONE game_over choice per set of four.

TIME ELAPSED — REQUIRED EVERY TURN:

- Recovery from severe setbacks requires sacrifice — time, resources, or relationships spent to undo damage.

The wooden chair screeches against the floor as you stand, fixing Finch with a hard stare. "We're done here." Zoe and Jayne rise instantly, their hands drifting toward their weapons. Kaylee looks nervously between you and Finch. Wash remains seated, sipping his drink casually but watching like a hawk.

Finch's eyes dart to the exit, then back to you. "Now, hold on, Captain Reynolds," he says, rising hastily. "No need to be hasty. This is a mutually beneficial arrangement."

You take a step toward the door, your crew following suit. The smoky air seems to thicken with tension. "Mutually beneficial?" you scoff. "You haven't been exactly forthcoming about what we're hauling."

Finch hesitates, glancing around the bar. His voice drops to a whisper. "All right, all right. It's... advanced tech. Unregistered. Let's just say the Alliance didn't exactly authorize its development."

Zoe raises an eyebrow. "And what happens when the Alliance finds out we're hauling it?"

Finch spreads his hands placatingly. "That's why we're keeping things discreet. You get paid handsomely for a simple run, and I get my cargo delivered. Everyone wins."

Jayne leans in, his voice low and dangerous. "Unless someone decides we're worth more dead than alive."

data.passage:
You lean forward, your voice low but intense. "Who else knows about this cargo, Finch?" The smoky air seems to thicken around you.

Finch shifts uncomfortably in his seat, his fingers drumming nervously on the table. "Well, Captain Reynolds, let's just say there are... interested parties. Some might say very interested."

Jayne's hand drifts toward his weapon, his eyes narrowing. "Interested parties? That sounds like trouble waiting to happen."

Finch raises his hands in a placating gesture. "Now, now, no need for that. Let's just say a certain Officer Bradshaw has her eye on this tech. But don't worry, I've got a plan to keep her off your tail."

Wash raises an eyebrow, glancing at you. "Officer Bradshaw? That doesn't sound like a minor inconvenience."

Zoe crosses her arms, her gaze piercing. "And what's your plan, exactly? Because if it involves us being the decoy, I might have objections."

Finch smiles, but it doesn't reach his eyes. "Nothing so dramatic, I assure you. Just a little... misdirection. You get the cargo to the rendezvous, I handle the rest."

A.text: Ask Finch directly if he’s setting you up with the Alliance.
A.consequence: Finch takes offense, potentially ending the deal and alerting Officer Bradshaw, causing alliance_alerted flag to become true and severe relationship damage with Finch.
A.narration_directive: Finch reacts angrily to the accusation, possibly signaling for backup or calling off the job, putting the crew in immediate danger with Alliance attention.

B.text: Demand more details about Officer Bradshaw’s involvement.
B.consequence: Finch provides more details about Bradshaw's corruption, increasing knowledge but raising his suspicion, leading to a further decrease in relationship with Finch and possible complications.
B.narration_directive: Finch hesitantly shares more about Bradshaw's bribes, but watches Mal closely for any sign of betrayal, adding tension to the negotiation.

C.text: Turn to the crew and ask if they’re still in favor of the job.
C.consequence: The crew discusses their willingness, revealing concerns and strengthening or weakening group cohesion, with potential small relationship adjustments among crew members.
C.narration_directive: The crew members voice their opinions on the job, with Zoe, Wash, Jayne, and Kaylee each weighing in based on their motivations, highlighting loyalties and doubts.

D.text: Agree to the job but warn Finch that betrayal won’t end well for him.
D.consequence: Mal agrees to the job, setting job_accepted flag to true, but Finch notes the warning and may adjust his betrayal plans, causing a further relationship decrease with Finch.
D.narration_directive: Mal accepts the job with a stern warning to Finch, who smiles thinly and outlines the next steps for the pickup, with the crew preparing to move out.

data.passage:
You glance at Zoe and nod toward a dimly lit alleyway. 'Think we oughta learn a bit more about our friend Finch before we commit fully,' you say. The crew follows as you slip into the shadows, heading toward a contact who owes you a favor.

The walk takes longer than expected, and by the time you reach your source—a grizzled informant named Tuck—night has fallen. Tuck's wary eyes dart around as he whispers, 'Finch's been runnin' jobs with the Crimson Suns. Word is, he's got Alliance eyes on him. You take this job, you'll be walkin' into a storm.'

Just as you're about to press for more, Wash mutters, 'We've got company.' You glance down the street to see two Alliance officers, including Officer Bradshaw, scanning the area with focused intensity. The crew exchanges uneasy looks—the evening just got a lot more complicated.

A.text: Confront Bradshaw head-on, bluff your way out of trouble
A.consequence: Mal's bluff fails, leading to a confrontation with Bradshaw that results in lost credits, damaged relationships, or alerting the Alliance further.
A.narration_directive: Mal attempts to bluff but is caught in a lie, forcing the crew into a costly escape.

B.text: Slip away quietly and head straight to Serenity to avoid detection
B.consequence: Mal and the crew slip away and reach Serenity, departing Persephone to start the job.
B.narration_directive: Mal successfully evades the officers and heads to Serenity, setting course for the rendezvous.

C.text: Send Jayne to distract the officers while the rest of you make a break for it
C.consequence: Jayne creates a distraction, allowing escape but drawing ire from the officers or putting Jayne at risk.
C.narration_directive: Jayne causes a commotion, diverting the officers while the crew slips away, but not without consequences.

D.text: Use the alleyways to lose the officers and regroup at a safe location
D.consequence: The crew uses the alleyways to evade the officers and regroup safely.
D.narration_directive: Mal leads the crew through the alleys, losing the officers without major incident.

data.passage:
You step forward with your best 'innocent merchant' smile, tipping your hat to Officer Bradshaw. 'Evenin', ma'am. Just some honest traders loading supplies. No need for—'

Bradshaw cuts you off with a sharp laugh. 'Malcolm Reynolds. I'd recognize that lying tongue anywhere.' She snaps her fingers, and a dozen Alliance troopers step from the shadows, rifles raised. 'You're coming with us.'

Jayne curses, already reaching for his weapon. Zoe grabs his arm with a warning look. Wash mutters, 'Well that went well,' as Kaylee frantically checks the alley exits. The troopers advance, their boots scraping against the cobblestones.

'Run!' you bark, drawing your revolver. The crew scatters like startled birds as the first energy bolts sizzle past your head. Crates topple as Jayne provides covering fire, his laughter ringing through the chaos. You can hear Bradshaw shouting orders over the commotion, her voice tight with fury.

A.text: Lead the crew through the sewer tunnels - stinky but Alliance-free
A.consequence: Crew escapes through sewers but loses time and may face hazards; possible minor inventory loss.
A.narration_directive: Describe the crew navigating the filthy sewers with comedic mishaps, evading the Alliance.

B.text: Split up to confuse pursuit, rendezvous at Serenity's dock
B.consequence: Crew successfully splits up and rendezvous at Serenity, departing Persephone; act advances to Act 2.
B.narration_directive: Show each crew member using clever tactics to evade pursuit, meeting at the dock, and Serenity taking off.

C.text: Have Kaylee trigger the diversion explosives she 'accidentally' planted
C.consequence: Explosives create a diversion for escape but draw more Alliance attention; potential relationship changes with Kaylee.
C.narration_directive: Kaylee triggers the explosives, causing chaos; the crew escapes but with increased notoriety.

D.text: Take Bradshaw hostage - risky but might clear a path
D.consequence: Hostage attempt fails, leading to a firefight that injures a crew member or causes significant resource loss.
D.narration_directive: Mal's risky move backfires; describe an intense shootout with casualties or losses.
